rationale: Route /profile/<user_id> uses user-controlled path param user_id to fetch users.get(user_id) with only a session-present check and no binding to session['user_id'], enabling viewing other users' profiles (IDOR).
Route /profile/<user_id>/export uses path param user_id to load users.get(user_id) and returns that user's data without verifying user_id matches the authenticated session user (IDOR data export).

Route /profile/<user_id>/transcript uses user-controlled user_id to generate and send a transcript file via send_file without checking user_id against session['user_id'], allowing unauthorized transcript download (IDOR).

Flask route /profile/<user_id>/transcript uses user-controlled path param user_id to generate and return a transcript file without verifying it matches session['user_id'] or otherwise authorizing access to that user's transcript.
Route /profile/<user_id>/export loads user = users.get(user_id) using a user-controlled path param and returns that user's data without any ownership check against the authenticated session user.

POST /profile/<user_id>/message takes user-controlled path param user_id and uses it to redirect to profile(user_id=...) without verifying it matches session['user_id'], enabling IDOR-style access chaining to another user's profile.

Flask route /profile/<user_id> uses user-controlled path param user_id to select user = users.get(user_id) with only a session-present check and no binding to session['user_id']/current user, enabling IDOR.

Route /profile/<user_id>/transcript uses user-controlled path param user_id to generate and return a transcript file named with that id via send_file without verifying user_id matches the authenticated session user.

Route /profile/<user_id>/export uses user-controlled path param user_id to load user = users.get(user_id) and returns that user's data without checking it belongs to session['user_id'] or an admin role.

POST /profile/<user_id>/message accepts user-controlled user_id in the path and redirects to profile(user_id=user_id) without ensuring it matches the authenticated session user, allowing cross-user interaction flows.
